steps.title: Compute the confidence interval
steps.content:
The margin of error creates a confidence interval around the sample mean: $$7 \pm 1.6 = [5.4, 8.6]$$.
Thus the lower bound is $$7 - 1.6 = 5.4$$ and the upper bound is $$7 + 1.6 = 8.6$$.

steps.title: Interpret the interval for the answer choices
steps.content:
A confidence interval of [5.4, 8.6] means it is plausible that the true population mean lies anywhere between 5.4 and 8.6 books. Only choice B states that the actual mean is between 5.4 and 8.6, which matches the interval.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
